title: rubus rhamnifolius subsp. maassii
intro: Rubus rhamnifolius subsp. maassii, also known as the Japanese blackthorn or Japanese thimbleberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus rhamnifolius species, which is a type of thorny shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Rubus rhamnifolius subsp. maassii may include the reduction of inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as the improvement of cardiovascular and immune system function. Rubus rhamnifolius subsp. maassii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional East Asian medicine for its purported health benefits.
